areas.name: Solace Township
areas.heading: AREA 1: Solace Township
areas.summary: Warm autumn Vallenwood treetown; ~500 people (not counting outlying farmers). No local government; ruled by Theocracy of Seekers from Haven (3 days west). Uses **Emas exchange system**. Gold bricks prop doors or hold down papers. Steel weapons carry a premium from smith Theros Ironfeld (most respected man in town).
areas.description:
Warm autumn Vallenwood treetown; ~500 people (not counting outlying farmers). No local government; ruled by Theocracy of Seekers from Haven (3 days west). Uses **Emas exchange system**. Gold bricks prop doors or hold down papers. Steel weapons carry a premium from smith Theros Ironfeld (most respected man in town).

All standard adventurer services available at reasonable rates. Exception: Theros charges extra for steel weapons.

areas.name: Inn of the Last Home
areas.heading: 1a. Inn of the Last Home
areas.summary: Read three descriptions depending on: approach, entry, and whether slavers are using module PCs.
areas.description:
Read three descriptions depending on: approach, entry, and whether slavers are using module PCs.

**NPCs:**

| NPC | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attack | Damage |
|-----|----|----|----|----|----|----|-----|
| Otik Sandath (Innkeeper) | LN | 12" | 6 | 10 | 1 | 1 | 1–4 |
| Tika Wayland (Barmaid) | NG | 12" | 16 | 9 | 3 | 1 | 1–6 (frying pan) |
| Townspeople ×5 | var | 12" | 3,4,5,8,8 | 10 | 1 | 1 | 1–4 |

**NPC reactions:** 50% friendly to any PC who approaches.

**Information available (each NPC gives one piece):**
- **Barkeeper (Otik):** Suspects the staff was forged by demons from Darken Wood
- **Man at table:** Hooded men have been asking about the staff; wonders if they offered a reward
- **Man by storyteller:** A Holy Guard rode through 2 days ago; anyone with the staff should make haste to Haven to meet the Prelate at the Temple there
- **Old Storyteller:** "I foresee great and terrible destiny in your eyes. There is a Blue Staff which you must return to Xak Tsaroth. There, in but a few days hence, you shall face your greatest peril in contest for the greatest gift given to man."
- **Girl at table:** Staff was probably found in Darken Wood; ruins there are full of wealth and dangers; no one who enters returns
- **Boy by fire:** Saw the White Stag near Prayer's Eye Peak a few days ago; "He who walks the paths of the white stag is blessed"

---

areas.name: New Haven Road
areas.heading: AREA 4: New Haven Road
areas.summary: Ancient stone bridge over Solace Stream. Road forks west/south. Concealed draconian ambush in trees, waiting for their leader (area 6) to sound a horn. PCs must climb a tree to spot them; even then only 30% chance.
areas.description:
Ancient stone bridge over Solace Stream. Road forks west/south. Concealed draconian ambush in trees, waiting for their leader (area 6) to sound a horn. PCs must climb a tree to spot them; even then only 30% chance.

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage | Notes |
|----------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|-------|
| Baaz Draconians ×8 | LE | 6"[15"]/18" | 3,6,9×2,10,12×2,16 | 4 | 2 | 1 or 2 | 1–8 or 1–4/1–4 | Turn to stone, crumble on death |

**If spotted:** All attack, sound horn, fill woods with battle cries. Dragon-men fight to the death; block retreat to Solace. Horn is a **warning** to other draconians, not a call for aid. They intend to remain disguised while in human lands. They are searching for the Crystal Staff but don't know why.

---

areas.name: Prayer's Eye Peak
areas.heading: AREA 5: Prayer's Eye Peak
areas.summary: Flaming autumn colors; Prayer's Eye Peak to southwest. **30% chance** one party member (random) spots the White Stag. If enemies pursue heroes into this area, chance increases to **80%**.
areas.description:
Flaming autumn colors; Prayer's Eye Peak to southwest. **30% chance** one party member (random) spots the White Stag. If enemies pursue heroes into this area, chance increases to **80%**.

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage |
|----------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|
| White Stag ×1 | LG | 24" | 77 | –5 | 10 | 3 | 1–12/1–6/1–6 |

**White Stag behavior:** Stays just ahead of heroes, leads them through the crack in Prayer's Eye Peak, then bounds at full speed into Darken Wood (area 24). Cannot be captured.

**If heroes kill the stag:** Dark thunderclouds form overhead; hover for 7 days; all players add +1 to their Armor Class (i.e., AC becomes worse by 1) during this time. Stag body disappears.

---

areas.name: Twin Flat
areas.heading: AREA 6: Twin Flat
areas.summary: Mountain valley; Crystalmir Lake to east; canyon westward. A group of 30–3,000 (1d100×10 feet) away: hooded figures pulling a large cart. See party at same moment.
areas.description:
Mountain valley; Crystalmir Lake to east; canyon westward. A group of 30–3,000 (1d100×10 feet) away: hooded figures pulling a large cart. See party at same moment.

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage | Notes |
|----------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|-------|
| Baaz Draconian Leader ×1 | LE | 6"[15"]/18" | 12 | 4 | 2 | 1 or 2 | 1–8 or 1–4/1–4 | Turns to stone, crumbles |
| Baaz Draconians ×10 | LE | 6"[15"]/18" | 2,3,5,6,8×2,9×2,10,11 | 4 | 2 | 1 or 2 | 1–8 or 1–4/1–4 | Turns to stone, crumbles |

**Disguise:** Hoods + black cloth masks + heavy gloves + leg wrappings. Despite autumn, overdressed.

**Leader's ruse:** Claims to be an old cleric; says their healing staff was stolen from Xak Tsaroth; a member of their flock is dying.

**If party claims ignorance:** Draconians step aside and let them pass.

**If party reveals they have/know about the staff:** Leader screams and grabs weapon; all draconians attack (limited to MV 6" because wings are bound). Each round, each draconian has 20% chance to tear off robes (uses action; frees them to move faster). Draconians try to drive party east into area 5 to sound horn and spring trap with area 4 draconians. Fight to the death.

---

areas.name: Jakanth Vale
areas.heading: AREA 7: Jakanth Vale
areas.summary: Unnatural stillness; heavily wrapped footprints lead to a campsite in a glade.
areas.description:
Unnatural stillness; heavily wrapped footprints lead to a campsite in a glade.

**Campsite:** Smells like burned hair. Charred bones in fire pit ashes. Grass stamped down.

**Treasure (searching):** Bright silver bracer with 4 gems (500 gpv each). Inside: "Firehawk, warrior of Que-Shu." Que-Shu Plainsmen identify this as the bracer of warriors charged with defending the tribe. These bands are forged around the warrior's arm — death alone removes them.

No combat encounter listed.

---

areas.heading: AREA 16: Haven's Vale
areas.summary: Six white towers rising from foothills. Refugees choking the road. Contingent of Seeker Guards riding up and down lines trying to keep order (and looking for the Crystal Staff).
areas.description:

areas.name: Lordcity of Haven
areas.heading: AREA 17: Lordcity of Haven
areas.summary: White marble towers trimmed in gold. Low decorative wall with six towers. Inner circle 1 mile across; 3 miles including outer homes. Normal population ~5,000 (now overflowing). Nearly all standard adventurer services available; ironsmith works only for Highseekers.
areas.description:
White marble towers trimmed in gold. Low decorative wall with six towers. Inner circle 1 mile across; 3 miles including outer homes. Normal population ~5,000 (now overflowing). Nearly all standard adventurer services available; ironsmith works only for Highseekers.

**Key sub-areas:** 17a Steel Tankard Tavern, 17b Councilhall of Highseekers.

City overflows with refugees (2–20 present everywhere; HD 1–3, AC 8–10).

**Holy Guards on every street corner (10 per encounter):** AC 4, HD 4, MV 12", Dmg 1–8. Most pressing mission: find bearer of the blue crystal staff and bring them before the Council.

areas.name: Steel Tankard Tavern
areas.heading: 17a. Steel Tankard Tavern
areas.summary: Packed shoulder to shoulder. Three notable figures:
areas.description:
Packed shoulder to shoulder. Three notable figures:
- **Man in fur cloak:** Doubts the Highseekers have any power; "Where is their magic? Where are their miracles?"
- **Forester:** Wonders why guards seek someone with a crystal staff; could it preserve them from invaders?
- **Woman with eye-patch:** All ways south are blocked. Qualinesti is closed. Darken Wood south entrance possible but no one returns. River White-rage between Darken Wood and Qualinesti is most dangerous. No happy choices.

---

areas.name: Councilhall of Highseekers
areas.heading: 17b. Councilhall of Highseekers
areas.summary: 50'×30'×30' tall hall. Nine chairs in semicircle on polished granite floor. Nine white-robed men with steel girdle bands.
areas.description:
50'×30'×30' tall hall. Nine chairs in semicircle on polished granite floor. Nine white-robed men with steel girdle bands.

**Entry:** 100 Holy Guardians (CG, MV 12", AC 4, HD 4, Dmg 1–8) encircle building. Admit only those who bear news of the blue crystal staff.

| NPC | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage |
|-----|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|
| Master of Highseekers ×1 | CG | 12" | 35 | 8 | 7 | 1 | 1–8 |
| Highseeker Councilors ×8 | CG | 12" | 25,26,17,22,26,16,15,16 | 8 | 5 | 1 | 1–8 |

**Situation:** Draconian army to the north offered not to invade if staff is returned to Xak Tsaroth. Council's prayers to Newgods go unanswered; no standing army. Crystal staff is their last hope.

**If party has staff and reveals it:** Councilors beg to take it. If refused, they beg party to return it to Xak Tsaroth.

**Crystal staff's reaction:** Any councilor who touches the staff is hit by a lightning bolt for 4d6 damage. After this, Highseekers declare it an evil artifact and command party to return it to Xak Tsaroth.

*(Note: The Master is too dignified to rise and touch the staff — determine randomly which councilor tries, 1d8.)*

---

areas.name: River White-rage
areas.heading: AREA 18: River White-rage
areas.summary: Frothing powerful river formed by joining of several rivers. Easy crossing at the fords; impassable elsewhere without a raft.
areas.description:
Frothing powerful river formed by joining of several rivers. Easy crossing at the fords; impassable elsewhere without a raft.

**Swimming rules:** Any PC attempting to walk or swim across (non-ford): STR check to swim back to starting side, or be swept 100' downstream taking 1d12 drowning damage. Must check again until they make the check or drown. PCs cannot be swept to the other side.

**Raft building:** 1d4 hours per PC the raft is built to carry (6 PCs = 6d4 hours). River is safe for rafts; floating downstream enters area 20.

---

areas.name: Qualinesti Elflands
areas.heading: AREA 19: Qualinesti Elflands
areas.summary: Thick aspen forest, trunks only a few feet apart. After more than 1 hour in area, 15 elves emerge and surround party.
areas.description:
Thick aspen forest, trunks only a few feet apart. After more than 1 hour in area, 15 elves emerge and surround party.

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage |
|----------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|
| Elves ×15 | LG | 12" | 17 each | 5 | 2+2 | 1 | 2–9 (sword) or 1–6 (arrow) |

**Behavior:** Bows nocked, swords drawn; demand surrender. Order: take PCs to centaurs (area 23). They do not attack first.

**If party fights:** Additional elf army units, 1 per game hour (identical stats). Fight to the death for their homeland, cold fierceness. They do not pursue beyond north border of their land.

**Why they have these orders:** "A glorious being passed through our land and said you would be coming. We do not know his name, but we have orders to take you to the edge of Darken Wood at his bidding."

---

areas.heading: AREA 20: The White-rage Cut
areas.summary: Tall cliffs funnel the river; crashing between two soaring bluffs.
areas.description:
Tall cliffs funnel the river; crashing between two soaring bluffs.

**Raft navigation:** Check both STR and DEX once per turn. Fail either = raft splinters on rocks; all passengers fall into rapids. Raft ends up nearer north shore; use swimming rules from area 18. North shore survivors find paths up cliff face into area 22. Raft moves 2 miles per turn.

---

areas.name: Magic Mountains
areas.heading: AREA 21: Magic Mountains
areas.summary: Mountain valley between Prayer's Eye Peak and second ridge. Tall aspens, still green into autumn. Soft grass carpet. Exits to SW and E lead into deeper woods.
areas.description:
Mountain valley between Prayer's Eye Peak and second ridge. Tall aspens, still green into autumn. Soft grass carpet. Exits to SW and E lead into deeper woods.

areas.name: Spirit Forest (Entry to Darken Wood)
areas.heading: AREA 22: Spirit Forest (Entry to Darken Wood)

source.heading: AREA 22: Spirit Forest (Entry to Darken Wood)

areas.summary: Thickening canopy; dappled light fades to nothing; black knotted trees.
areas.description:
Thickening canopy; dappled light fades to nothing; black knotted trees.

**Darken Wood confusion effect:** PCs who enter areas 22–26 are subject to its confusing charm. When leaving the Darken Wood, PCs **cannot express** what they saw or experienced there even though their memory is clear.

**Spectral Minion challenge:** PCs see strange shapes flitting through dense woods. Minions challenge them — ask their names.
- If they **answer falsely:** Spectral Minions attack
- If they **give their correct names OR show Crystal Staff:** Minions halt and let them pass

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage | Notes |
|----------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|-------|
| Spectral Minions ×12 | CE | 30" | 17×3,18×2,19×2,14×2,10×2,12 | 2 | 3 | 1 | 1–8 | 20% magic resist |

**If allowed to pass:** Leader says, "The Forestmaster awaits. You are expected." One spirit escorts them ~½ mile to area 26. Escort's backstory: "Ages ago, before the world changed, we were ordered to keep this land safe. That is the Purpose. We failed. Now we repent by protecting the land while we can."

---

areas.heading: AREA 25: Starnight Canyon
areas.summary: Craggy canyons among Sentinel Mountains peaks. **15% chance per turn** to encounter Pegasi. They graze calmly; PCs can mount them.

| Pegasi ×1–12 | CG | 24"/48" | — | 6 | 4 | 3 | 1–8/1–8/1–3 |

**Once mounted:** Pegasi bound into sky and fly directly to area 26 regardless of what riders try to direct them to do. If rider threatens a Pegasus: creature bucks once in the air; rider must check DEX or fall.

areas.name: Unicorn Grove
areas.heading: AREA 26: Unicorn Grove
areas.summary: Dense oak forest opens into a glade. Soft green grass up a hillside; rock outcropping at top. Forestmaster Unicorn stands atop the overhang, head raised high, strange sadness in its eyes.
areas.description:
Dense oak forest opens into a glade. Soft green grass up a hillside; rock outcropping at top. Forestmaster Unicorn stands atop the overhang, head raised high, strange sadness in its eyes.

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage |
|----------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|
| Forestmaster Unicorn ×1 | CG | 24" | 60 | 2 | 10 | 3 | 1–8/1–8/1–20 |

**Forestmaster's greeting:** "I am the Forestmaster. You have entered my Great Forest: I grant you passage and the aid of all creatures while you remain within our borders." From now until PCs leave the forest: no creature attacks unless PCs attack first.

**Forestmaster's message (from a great and glorious being who visited days ago):** "You must fly straight away across the Eastwall Mountains. Within two days you must be within Xak Tsaroth. There, if you prove worthy, you shall receive the greatest gift given to the world."

**The Forestmaster knows:** A terrible force of evil will shortly destroy the beauty of his domain.

**If PCs request help:** Forestmaster summons one Pegasus per PC. They circle silently overhead, land gently, allow mounting, then hurtle into the air and soar over the glade. PCs looking back see Forestmaster call after them.

**Upon leaving Darken Wood:** PCs subject to confusion of leaving (cannot express what they experienced). Must also make CON check each turn or fall asleep.

**If party attacks the Forestmaster:** He calls at once for magical forest creatures to defend him. Roll immediately on the Darken Wood Random Encounter table. From now on all creatures encountered attack on sight; if headed out of the woods, creatures only try to chase them out.

---

**Area 27:** Gateway Pass — worn road peacefully between Sentinel Peak spires; brilliant leaves floating.

**Area 28:** Gateway town — comfortable houses and shops at base of Sentinel Mountains. Buildings on the ground (unlike Solace). No ironsmithy. Citizens know nothing of danger from demons or evil armies; dismiss such talk as tall tales — "Dragons in the night" is their name for stories of things that don't exist.

**Nightshade's story (if rescued):** From village of Que-Teh; dragonmen taking everyone south as slaves; secret council while chained; he was chosen to escape while comrades created a distraction; a tremendous evil army lies to the south; second army waits to crush the Seeker Lands from the north; they hold back their armies only for fear that the crystal staff will return to Xak Tsaroth. areas.summary: Ironclaw tree roots twist into mud and swamp water. Vines, trees, and ferns block vision beyond a few feet. Ground soaked and spongy. All in shadow.

**Tracks:** Many creatures passed through within last 3–4 hours. Faintly reptilian prints. Heroes who have met draconians: 20% chance to recognize them.

**What they know:** Crystal staff is a very powerful item; stolen from the ruins; the one thing their leader Verminaard fears can upset his plans.

areas.name: Temple of Baaz (Draconian Camp)
areas.heading: AREA 44f: Temple of Baaz (Draconian Camp)
areas.summary: Massive ruins, small broken towers. Ferns and bushes through flagstone plaza with small shacks and lean-tos. Huge bonfire at north end. Beyond fire: crumbled dome with a **huge black dragon** figure with wings outfolded.
areas.description:
Massive ruins, small broken towers. Ferns and bushes through flagstone plaza with small shacks and lean-tos. Huge bonfire at north end. Beyond fire: crumbled dome with a **huge black dragon** figure with wings outfolded.

**Dragon is a wicker model.** However, draconians nearby attack any unescorted intruders.

**If PCs are prisoners:** Equipment placed randomly in one of six huts; PCs held in bamboo cage.

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage | Spells | Notes |
|----------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|--------|-------|
| Bozak Draconian Priest ×1 | LE | 6"[15"]/18" | 23 | 2 | 4 | 1 or 2 | 1–8 or 1–4/1–4 | 1st: charm, MM, shield; 2nd: invis, mirror image | Explodes on death |
| Baaz Draconians ×7 | LE | 6"[15"]/18" | 8×2,9,5,11,10,3 | 4 | 2 | 1 or 2 | 1–8 or 1–4/1–4 | — | Turn to stone, crumble |

**Priest behavior:** Runs the camp and conducts worship before the wicker dragon. Orders PCs held for 3 days until Khisanth (the priest's commander) comes to claim them. If party has the staff: "The crystal staff is the greatest danger to our masters we know." If no staff: asks where it is. Either way tells party: "Now that you are here, we only hope that you do not die as easily as those of Que-Shu did at our hands."

**The Wicker Dragon — Trick:**
- Opening at base between its legs; main wing braces tied together just below mouth; mouth forms a funnel
- If a PC gets inside unnoticed, leaps on main brace, and shouts through the mouth: panics draconians and drives them screaming into jungle for 1d6 turns
- **30% chance** the structure falls face-first into the bonfire and burns furiously
- Anyone inside must check DEX to escape; takes 1d6 damage each round DEX check is failed

**The Bamboo Cage:**
- 1 Baaz draconian guards it night and day
- 10% chance per daylight hour the guard falls asleep; 30% chance per hour after sundown
- Successful bend bars/lift gates check breaks the bars open

---

areas.name: Plaza of Death
areas.heading: AREA 44k: Plaza of Death
areas.summary: Broad cobblestone street, fluted columns, relief carvings of sagging buildings. Large cobblestone courtyard with four tall free-standing columns. In front of pillars: a **huge well** plunging into the earth, vapors rising. To the north: a single building stands whole (Temple of Mishakal, area 46).
areas.description:
Broad cobblestone street, fluted columns, relief carvings of sagging buildings. Large cobblestone courtyard with four tall free-standing columns. In front of pillars: a **huge well** plunging into the earth, vapors rising. To the north: a single building stands whole (Temple of Mishakal, area 46).

**The Well:** Dark and terrible stillness. Gazing into it: nothing but vast blackness. Rushing air, dead cold, swells from below.

**Dragon Arrival:** Suddenly Khisanth bursts from the well and unfolds tremendous leather wings. She casts **Darkness** covering the entire area.

**Khisanth's Surface Battle Tactics:**
1. First combat round: gains speed in air, circles for better attack position
2. PCs susceptible to dragon fear (see Monster Manual p. 30)
3. Attacks from the air while hovering; avoids swords and hand weapons
4. Bombards heroes with **magic missile** and **sleep** spells
5. Makes 2 aerial passes using 64-hp breath weapon (acid) each time
6. On 3rd pass: feels need for safety of lair; flies over well, folds wings, drops like stone down the well; breaks out of dive far below; moves toward lair

**After this encounter:** PCs may explore the well further (area 45). Heroes may also enter the building to the north (area 46a).

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage | Spells |
|----------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|--------|
| Khisanth (Onyx), Ancient Huge Black Dragon | CE | 12"/24" | 64 | 3 | 8 | 3 | 1–4/1–4/3–18 | 1st: charm person ×2, magic missile ×2, shocking grasp ×2, sleep ×2 |

**Breath weapon:** Acid. Damage = current HP total. Use twice in this encounter.

**Temple doors (north building):** Gold doors weighing 15,000 gpw each. Counterpoised hinges; open as normal doors. Only entrance to the windowless temple.

areas.name: The Great Well
areas.heading: AREA 45: The Great Well
areas.summary: The shaft plunges into darkness, foul air rising. Steam and heavy odor of rottenness. The masonry shaft drops **100 feet** before opening into the ceiling of a vast cavern. On the cavern floor **800 feet below** lie the shattered remains of Xak Tsaroth — the most preserved part of the ruined ancient capital. The well sits directly over the central plaza of the ruins (area 67).

areas.name: Mishakal's Form
areas.heading: 46b. Mishakal's Form
areas.summary: Polished statue of singular grace — a woman draped in flowing robes, hair cascading about shoulders, neck adorned with a detailed amulet. Expression: radiant hope tempered with sadness. Feeling of warmth and love pervades the room.
areas.description:
Polished statue of singular grace — a woman draped in flowing robes, hair cascading about shoulders, neck adorned with a detailed amulet. Expression: radiant hope tempered with sadness. Feeling of warmth and love pervades the room.

**The statue:** Likeness of **Mishakal, ancient goddess of healing**. Her power still fills the chamber.

| Entity | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage |
|--------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|
| Mishakal, Goddess of Healing | LG | 24"/48" | 355 | –3 | 25 | 2 | 3–24 |

**Trigger:** If a cleric brings the blue crystal staff into this room, the statue comes to life and speaks.

**Mishakal's message:** The gods have not turned away from man — man turned away from them and now seeks gods who do not exist. The end of darkness nears. Krynn faces its greatest test. The Disks of Mishakal lie far below this temple, in the lair of the dragon. Recover the Disks. Present the staff boldly and never waver — then you shall prevail. The journey is not complete until you also search for a true Leader of the People.

**Staff recharging:** Statue's arms are curved as though they once held a long thin pole. Placing the crystal staff in the statue's arms **glows brightly and recharges** it to full.

Two 10'-diameter holes in the floor. Huge black iron pot on chain over the northern hole. Chain runs around two large wheels across the ceiling and down through the southern hole. Two draconian overseers guard.

**The holes drop 700 feet** to area 66.

**Elevator cycle:** Every 3 game turns, gong sounds far below; overseers crack whip; 30 Aghar clamber into the pot; pot descends slowly; another pot of draconians groans up through the southern hole.

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage | Notes |

**If attacked:** Overseers panic and leap into the pot. PCs can jump aboard; heavier = faster descent. More than 2 PCs = pot falls quickly (warn players).

**Second pot (rising):** Contains 6 Baaz Draconians (hp 16 each, AC 4, HD 2) — shock troops, fight to the death.

areas.name: Drain
areas.heading: 58b. Drain
areas.summary: Steepening floor narrows into a smooth, yard-wide **vertical drain**. Water collected from all the cavern fissures swirls steadily down the sides of the shaft.
areas.description: Steepening floor narrows into a smooth, yard-wide **vertical drain**. Water collected from all the cavern fissures swirls steadily down the sides of the shaft.

areas.name: Down the Sink
areas.heading: 58c. Down the Sink
areas.summary: Water splashes down slick sides of the vertical shaft. Chilly; noise of the water is deafening.
areas.description:
Water splashes down slick sides of the vertical shaft. Chilly; noise of the water is deafening.

---

areas.name: Windows Below
areas.heading: 59a. Windows Below
areas.summary: Natural shaft of smooth rock carries a spray of water into a cramped cave. Ceiling filled with water. Floor is made of solid masonry stonework — what appears to be a **30' tall window laid into the stone floor**. Glass is gone; ironwork of the window-frame remains.

areas.name: Dance on the Wall
areas.heading: 59b. Dance on the Wall
areas.summary: A vast hall lying **on its side**: its northern wall (beamed like a ceiling) is one side; its southern wall (tiled floor) is another. Now the ceiling and floor of the room are windowed. Water pours in from upper windows, out through broken lower ones. Mosses and fungus everywhere. Fungus on the floor **appears to be moving**.
areas.description:
A vast hall lying **on its side**: its northern wall (beamed like a ceiling) is one side; its southern wall (tiled floor) is another. Now the ceiling and floor of the room are windowed. Water pours in from upper windows, out through broken lower ones. Mosses and fungus everywhere. Fungus on the floor **appears to be moving**.

**The curtains:** From the northern end of each upper window (the former top), curtains of corroded woven metal hang down to the wall below. Support the weight of a man. Those from the westernmost window have been pulled over to an arched opening in the west wall, 30' up, fastened there — **this opening leads to a dark corridor**.

**Crossing from curtain to curtain:** Each adventurer must make a DEX check or fall to the floor (3d6 damage) and be attacked by the snakes.

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage | Notes |
|----------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|-------|
| Poisonous Snakes ×10 | N | 15" | 9 each | 6 | 2+1 | 1 | 1 hp + poison (3d4 pts) | On the floor |

areas.name: Entrance to the Hall of Justice
areas.heading: AREA 60: Entrance to the Hall of Justice

source.heading: AREA 60: Entrance to the Hall of Justice

areas.summary: Only dim light from above scattering darkness onto ruins-covered cavern floor. A facade of an ancient building juts sideways from the cavern wall. Its roof lies half-buried in rubble to the north; steps now rising to the south. Delicately carved pillars lie broken amid rubble sloping west to a cobblestone street. Further west: ring of broken stones — foundation of a tremendous tower. The tower itself lies next to the foundation, its top buried in the cavern wall. Water pours into the cavern from several high places.

areas.name: North Cavern Falls
areas.heading: AREA 61: North Cavern Falls
areas.summary: Water thunders down from an opening high up the cavern wall. Gleaming faintly, tumbles over rubble, forms a river on the cobblestone street, rushing out of sight.
areas.description:
Water thunders down from an opening high up the cavern wall. Gleaming faintly, tumbles over rubble, forms a river on the cobblestone street, rushing out of sight.

areas.name: Treasury Tower
areas.heading: AREA 62: Treasury Tower
areas.summary: A broken foundation circles where a tremendous tower once stood. **Two 10' cubes** stand within the circle, apparently untouched by the catastrophe. Each cube has an arched opening in its side; the openings face each other, 20' apart. The fallen tower lies on its side, its top buried in the cavern wall to the north. Inside the hollow cylinder of the tower: only darkness.
areas.description:
A broken foundation circles where a tremendous tower once stood. **Two 10' cubes** stand within the circle, apparently untouched by the catastrophe. Each cube has an arched opening in its side; the openings face each other, 20' apart. The fallen tower lies on its side, its top buried in the cavern wall to the north. Inside the hollow cylinder of the tower: only darkness.

**The cubes:** Permanent dimension doors, both leading to the vault (57b). Warp space so they appear to be normal arched entrances.

areas.name: North Mall
areas.heading: AREA 63: North Mall
areas.summary: Catastrophe has marred once-beautiful buildings. Moss-covered rubble hides cobblestones. Icy river surges northwest; the street ends abruptly in a **chasm** — mist billows from the hole, water crashes somewhere below.
areas.description:
Catastrophe has marred once-beautiful buildings. Moss-covered rubble hides cobblestones. Icy river surges northwest; the street ends abruptly in a **chasm** — mist billows from the hole, water crashes somewhere below.

**Crossing the swift water (within 50' of the chasm):** Must check both STR and DEX to cross unaided. Fail either: swept 10' toward the twisting whirlpool in the crevasse. Must check again or be swept another 10' toward the maelstrom. Falling into the crevasse: drowns unless waterbreathing, in which case PC surfaces at the bottom of a **1,000' cliff** floating out into the Newsea.

areas.heading: 64a. Entry
areas.summary: Dark stains on walls. 10'-wide hall curves before a set of bronze double doors. In front of the doors: mosses cover an ancient, faded mural.
areas.description:
Dark stains on walls. 10'-wide hall curves before a set of bronze double doors. In front of the doors: mosses cover an ancient, faded mural.

**The mural:** Heroes must clean it off before realizing it's a map of a city. Even discovered, only 30% chance of recognizing their current location on the map. Anyone who figures out which part is the cavern area can discover the way to the Great Plaza (67), the Hall of Justice (70a), and the Treasury Tower (62).

Dark room; echo suggests it is large. From deep in the darkness: shrill, hiccuping sounds.

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage | Spells | Notes |
|----------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|--------|-------|
| Bozak Draconian (Drunk) ×1 | LE | 6"[15"]/18" | 15 | 2 | 4 | 1 or 2 | 1–8 or 1–4/1–4 | 1st: charm, MM, push; 2nd: darkness 15', web | Explodes 1d6 on death |

**Stone drunk and lying in northwest corner.** Thinks adventurers are his draconian brothers; babbles in common tongue without trouble. Says: "The Captain an' I was havin' a little celebration when we got a bit too celebrated! I remember that a bunch of stinking gully dwarves came along and carried him off. If they let the humans in on our real plan, the whole thing could be ruined. I'm pretty sure they dragged him off to their Guildhouse west of here. I've got to get a rescue party together — just as soon as I've had a chance to sleep...off...this..."

**Cannot be awakened for at least 3 hours.**

**Conversation overheard:**
- First draconian: Wants to know why they're waiting; can just crush the humans
- Second draconian: If Verminaard needs a staff, let him get it; Khisanth guards the only writings of the true gods — without them humans can't stop the draconian armies

areas.name: Court of Reception
areas.heading: AREA 66: Court of Reception
areas.summary: Thin shaft of light overhead dimly outlines a dismal 40'-diameter courtyard. Broken cobblestone streets run east, west, and south. Against the northern wall: a **10' high wicker dragon** bearing a large round metal plate (gong) in a hole in its chest. A robed figure stands beside it. A huge black metal pot sits in the center; a chain rises from the pot disappearing into the mists above.
areas.description:
Thin shaft of light overhead dimly outlines a dismal 40'-diameter courtyard. Broken cobblestone streets run east, west, and south. Against the northern wall: a **10' high wicker dragon** bearing a large round metal plate (gong) in a hole in its chest. A robed figure stands beside it. A huge black metal pot sits in the center; a chain rises from the pot disappearing into the mists above.

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage | Notes |
|----------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|-------|
| Baaz Draconian ×1 | LE | 6"[15"]/18" | 16 | 4 | 2 | 1 or 2 | 1–8 or 1–4/1–4 | Turn to stone, crumble |

**Within a few minutes:** 8 more draconians arrive (hp 2,3,5×2,8×2,9,12).

**The gong:** Robed draconian takes a mallet and hits the metal plate. Moments after the sound, the chain grows taut; the pot begins to rise with 8 draconians scrambling in. A second pot (bristling with Aghar arms and legs) descends on a chain and passes the ascending one.

**Second pot (descending):** Contains 15 Gully Dwarves (Slud Clan Aghar; hp 8 each; AC 8; HD 2). They wait until the robed draconian yells "Now, you scum! Climb out or else!" — then leap out and scramble through area 59b and 58.

areas.name: Great Plaza
areas.heading: AREA 67: Great Plaza
areas.summary: A single column of light pierces the overhead mists onto a vast plaza at least **120 feet across**. Steady streams from east and west streets flow into a large pool emptying into a north river. The roar of waterfalls fades.
areas.description:
A single column of light pierces the overhead mists onto a vast plaza at least **120 feet across**. Steady streams from east and west streets flow into a large pool emptying into a north river. The roar of waterfalls fades.

**Dragon arrives:** A huge black creature arches like a cobra on the steps of one of the southern buildings. The plaza stills. Khisanth speaks in a hissing language to a draconian; the draconian orders Aghar to begin a search for an escaped prisoner (at area 67b). Then the dragon retreats through area 70a into its lair (70k).

**Encounter:**

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage | Spells |
|----------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|--------|
| Khisanth (Onyx) ×1 | CE | 12"/24" | 64 | 3 | 8 | 3 | 1–4/1–4/3–18 | 1st ×8: charm person ×2, MM ×2, shocking grasp ×2, sleep ×2 |
| Gully Dwarves (Slud Clan Aghar) ×7 | CN | 12" | 2,3,5,6,8×2,9 | 8 | 2 | 1 or 2 | 1–6 or 1–4/1–4 | — |
| Bozak Leader ×1 | LE | 6"[15"]/18" | 15 | 4 | 4 | 1 or 2 | 1–8 or 1–4/1–4 | 1st: charm, MM, push; 2nd: darkness 15', web |

**The escaped prisoner:** Aghar scatter to search for a Que-Shu tribeswoman hiding at area 67b. Draconians follow one search group randomly.

**Draconian captain:** Stripped of weapons and uniform; woke up with a horrible hangover.

**If untied:** Uses every means (mainly spells) to escape and regain command of troops. **Spells:** 1st: charm person, magic missile, push; 2nd: darkness 15', web.

**What the captain reveals (only if threatened with death or charmed):** "Verminaard is our Dragonlord! True cleric of evil, he calls upon the powers of darkness to bring this land under his rule and that of his brothers. Yet he fears one thing: the uprising of light against the darkness. We guard that which might kindle hope among men and lift the darkness! We guard the true knowledge of the gods. Under the eye of Khisanth, the truth is safe in the secret night!"

**The dwarves:** Try to flee when they notice heroes (after only 2 rounds).

areas.heading: 68i. Messy Mess
areas.summary: Lukewarm, stinking glop flying about. Bowls shattering. Knives clattering. Gully dwarves settling a wild argument over dinner.
areas.description:
Lukewarm, stinking glop flying about. Bowls shattering. Knives clattering. Gully dwarves settling a wild argument over dinner.

**Each round a hero is in this room:** 70% chance a bowl of goo hits and splatters on them. Aghar stew made by throwing nearly anything dead or near-death into a pot. **Stench:** Wears off in 1d20 turns; during that time other heroes must check CON to stand within 10' of the affected hero. Neither the affected hero nor anyone with him has a chance of surprising an enemy.

Aghar don't notice heroes until 5 rounds after entering. They throw food at intruders (chance to hit doesn't increase; they're too busy dodging bowls themselves). **If heroes try to fight:** Foul-smelling dwarves run out one of the doors.

| Highbulp Guards (Bulp Clan) ×4 | CN | 12" | 4,6,7,13 | 8 | 2 | 1 or 2 | 1–6 or 1–4/1–4 |

**Guards:** Attack only if they or Phudge are attacked. Though Aghar generally run away from a fight, **when backed into a corner they are ferocious.**

**Phudge's disposition:** Dislikes draconians. Misses the times when Aghar could go for weeks without working. Dragonmen have cleaned the place up far too much for his tastes.

**What Phudge knows:** The Disks of Mishakal lie in the dragon's lair at 70k; also knows about the tunnel from 69b into that lair.

**What Phudge offers:** Guides to various places in the caverns. No other aid.

areas.name: The Secret Way
areas.heading: 69b. The Secret Way
areas.summary: Rivulets of water trickle down the walls.
areas.description:
Rivulets of water trickle down the walls.

**Finding the trapdoor:** PCs who check the floor near the center of the room find a closed trapdoor operated by a pull ring. Add the STR of all PCs trying to pull the door open to find the **percentage chance of success**.

**The trapdoor:** 4'-square opening over a 4'-square shaft 20' deep. Opens into a 5'-diameter masonry tunnel half-filled with water.

**Before entering:** Check for a random encounter. If indicated, roll 1d20 for how many rounds later the encounter takes place. Treat a Khisanth encounter as **no encounter** (she's not down here).

**The tunnel layout:**
- North of the access shaft: runs 30' to a cave-in
- South from the shaft: runs 200' south, then turns east 340' to another cave-in
- **30 feet before the end of the east branching tunnel:** A shaft ascends 30' to a **grate in the floor of Khisanth's lair (70k)**

---

areas.description:
A vast chamber, **100' in diameter**, 4 stories tall, cracked and broken translucent dome above. Dim light filters through mists above and spreads onto the center of the rotunda. There: a **glossy black dragon** straddles a pile of jewels, steel weapons, and intricate items.

**This is Khisanth's lair.** She is duty-bound to keep the Disks of Mishakal from the PCs.

| Creature | AL | MV | HP | AC | HD | Attacks | Damage | Spells |
|----------|----|----|----|----|----|---------|----|--------|
| Khisanth (Onyx), Ancient Huge Black Dragon | CE | 12"/24" | 64 | 3 | 8 | 3 | 1–4/1–4/3–18 | 1st: charm person ×2, MM ×2, shocking grasp ×2, sleep ×2 |

**Combat:** If she has breath weapon or spells remaining, she takes to the air and uses them. Otherwise she must fight on the ground.

**Crystal Staff in the lair:** Any PC holding the staff hears a soft feminine whisper: "Retrieve the disks, and hit the dragon with this staff. All will be well."

**If PC manages to hit the dragon with the staff:**
> The staff shatters as it strikes the dragon. Blue light bursts from the staff in brilliant spherical waves. The figure who broke the staff is lost in light, disappearing as a ringing sound fills the chamber. The wall of shining blue pulses, its power shaking the earth itself. The great dragon is covered in the glow and falls, screaming, into light. The walls shake; pillars in the room sway and topple. The cracked ceiling begins to crumble. Above the falling ceiling, the cavern walls themselves begin to collapse.

**Cave-in consequences:**
- All creatures in all encounter areas are now trying desperately to escape the collapsing cavern
- PCs: **15% chance per turn** of being hit by falling ceiling debris for **1d12 damage**
- Water level in cavern rises **1 foot per turn**
- If heroes escape: direct them to Temple of Mishakal (46b) and read the Epilogue

**Dragon's hoard:**
- The **Disks of Mishakal** (see DL1_08)
- 56 gems at 200 gp value each
- A **cloak of invisibility**
- 1,000 platinum pieces
- A **ring of darkness** (projects darkness to a 100' radius)

---

## EPILOGUE

**If PCs break the staff, escape the cavern, and return to area 46b:**

The beautiful statue of the goddess towers above. In her hands lies a perfect carved likeness of the blue crystal staff. At the foot of the statue, a lone figure stirs.

**The lone figure:** The person who broke the staff. They lie here unharmed, all hit points restored. The staff is now part of the statue. A **platinum version of the Medallion of Faith** — bearing the symbol of the goddess — now adorns the neck of the PC who broke the staff.

**If PCs took the Disks without the Crystal Staff (didn't use it):** Allow them to make their way out of the cavern as best they can. Do not read the Epilogue box.

**Continuing to DL2:** If players continue with Dragons of Flame (DL2): the day ends as heroes trudge back over the Forsaken Mountains. Dark clouds flash in the west — but the billowing darkness is more than clouds. Smoke climbs into the autumn sky, rising from the **forests of Solace**.
